2016-Profiles_II-Street-Paste_up-Porto.Title: Profiles II
2016-Profiles_II-Street-Paste_up-Porto.Private Notes: Old description: "After a while I started to devote a little more time to the Profiles pieces, adding frames and other embellishments."

2016-Profiles-Street-Paste_up-Porto.Title: Profiles
2016-Profiles-Street-Paste_up-Porto.Private Notes: "The Profiles series was initially just a series of quick sketches I was doing to pass the time. They slowly took on a life of their own, and I started pasting them up around town. It's a fun, relaxed project and the results are simple, intuitive characters."

2016-Satan's_Mascara-Street-Paste_up-Por.Title: Satan's Mascara
2016-Satan's_Mascara-Street-Paste_up-Por.Private Notes:
"Satan's Mascara features unique paste-ups, hand painted with acrylic and pastel onto brown packing paper.

The project was inspired in part by drag culture and the contradictions in gender perception. I wanted to create images that blur the lines between masculine and feminine, exploring the dichotomy of powerfully masculine femininity."

2017-Ladies-Street-Paste_up-Various.Title: Ladies
2017-Ladies-Street-Paste_up-Various.Private Notes: "These pieces aren't necessarily part of a coherent series, but evolved out of the Profiles series, and have a sort of common format. The pieces are produced quickly and intuitively on simple newsprint, which is thin enough to really hold the form of the wall, and yellows and ages nicely in the sun."

2017-Skin_Deep-Studio-Painting.Title: Skin Deep
2017-Skin_Deep-Studio-Painting.Private Notes:
Skin Deep is a series of paintings exploring the image of sensuality that we portray publicly and privately. Certain pieces have hidden images on the reverse, never seen by the viewer.

The pieces are very delicate in their aesthetic, the torsos are more decorative than aggressively sexual. The raw wood adds a beautiful sensuality to the work, complementing the simplicity of the imagery.

It was exhibited in Porto in 2018.
The pieces are available individually, or in groupings.
Contact info@berriblue.com for details.

2017-The_Cats-Studio-Painting.Title: The Cats
2017-The_Cats-Studio-Painting.Private Notes:
This piece features imagery very common in BerriBlue's street art.

The piece is bright and playful, and is a beautiful object. The raw, unprimed plywood adds a beautiful, natural quality, and grounds the piece.

The nude figures are energetic and unabashed. being cropped adds a touch of intrigue, but also de-personifies the figures so they are more abstract, rather than being characters.

2018-A_Family_Portrait-Studio-Painting.Title: A Family Portrait
2018-A_Family_Portrait-Studio-Painting.Private Notes:
This piece is about BerriBlue's difficult relationship with her family. The symbolism in the work is very personal to her. At the age of 13, Berri left Poland for Dublin. This move meant that she was totally cut off from her family and her roots in Gdansk.

The relationship she has with her immediate family is very strained, and casts her childhood in a painful light. The blueberries have been uprooted and lie scattered, and a bulbous, cancerous growth has been cut away, lying discarded. The only living thing is the Iris, now sprouting new roots and creating its own optimistic future, isolated from the rest.

2018-A_Letter_to_my_Mother-Studio-Painti.Title: A Letter to my Mother
2018-A_Letter_to_my_Mother-Studio-Painti.Private Notes:
This piece is very personal to the artist, and explores the psychological impact of re-opening contact with her estranged mother.

The disembodied hand, showing the fresh wounds of self-harm, stands separated from the composition below, which portrays the both beautiful and painful nature of the relationship of the artist with her mother. The powerfully visceral imagery here conveys a sense of inseparability between the artists' self, and her past.

Note the sharp frontier between the blue and gold. The cuts end abruptly as the wounded hand reaches across this border, and the splash of green behind implies that something new is growing. The act of reaching out again is shown as an optimistic, if painful one.

2018-How_It_Feels-Studio-Painting.Title: How It Feels
2018-How_It_Feels-Studio-Painting.Private Notes:
This piece was the artist's attempt to depict the sensation of a strong psychotic episode. BerriBlue's borderline personality disorder frequently causes unpleasant, disturbing thoughts and sensations. The carnal, flesh and blood nature of her body, bones and skin become particularly hard to deal with as she becomes more and more dissociative towards herself and the reality around her.

The piece portrays this disturbing sensation as something visceral, painful, but also beautiful. It is currently in a private collection in California. Another version of the piece is in Porto, as a street art mural.

2018-My_Golden_Girl-Studio-Painting.Title: My Golden Girl
2018-My_Golden_Girl-Studio-Painting.Private Notes:
This piece is a semi self portrait, depicting BerriBlue as the mother figure, holding Muszka, her little chihuahua. As well as a sweet, simple figurative image, the piece also portrays the maternal connection between mother and child, and, of course, owner and pet.

The piece is now in a private collection in California.

2018-My_Relationship_with_Time-Studio-Pa.Title: My Relationship with Time
2018-My_Relationship_with_Time-Studio-Pa.Private Notes:
This piece was originally exhibited in Porto, in the 2018 exhibition "Tension". It is now in a private collection.

The piece explores mortality, and the sharp divide that people seem to see between the living and the dead. Here they are shown as one continuous stream, no different from one another, except in state. Those who have departed depicted as skulls, and the phallic motif representing the carnality of the living.

2018-Painting_Through_Psychosis-Studio-P.Title: Painting Through Psychosis
2018-Painting_Through_Psychosis-Studio-P.Private Notes:
This self portrait was painted during an intense psychotic episode. The frankness with which BerriBlue approaches mental health and self harm is beautiful and haunting.

The figure appears vulnerable and flawed, and there is no sense of body positivity. The pose and the background imagery imply a sense of defensive confusion, but the eyes lock piercingly with the viewer.

This piece was chosen as the chief promotional image for an event in Porto's Miguel Bombarda arts district in 2018.

2018-Remains-Studio-Painting.Title: Remains
2018-Remains-Studio-Painting.Private Notes: Remains was first exhibited in Porto, in the 2018 exhibition "Tension".  The piece is painted on raw, unprimed plywood, with much of the natural grain visible. The lustrous gold finish is offset by the simple material. The imagery is bold and instinctive, and the frantic nature of the piece's creation is clear to see.  An expression of the physicality of being alive. After death, there are remains. But for something to remain, something else must be gone.

2018-Retrospect-Studio-Painting.Title: Retrospect
2018-Retrospect-Studio-Painting.Private Notes:
This piece deals with heartbreak and retrospection. How the things that may have hurt us in the past become a crucial part of ourselves.

The visceral imagery is slightly disturbing, but also has a certain beauty and playfulness. The natural grain of the plywood shows through the lustrous gold, giving the piece a rich, organic feel.

2018-Scoliosis-Studio-Painting.Title: Scoliosis
2018-Scoliosis-Studio-Painting.Private Notes:
This double-sided piece is an attempt to depict the pain and discomfort of scoliosis. The twisted figure is covered by broad expressive strokes, and the plywood board is marred by twisted, scratched carvings.
The figures bones are hidden on the back of the piece, unseen, yet impactful.

2018-Self_Portrait_(Psychosis)-Studio-Pa.Title: Self Portrait (Psychosis)
2018-Self_Portrait_(Psychosis)-Studio-Pa.Private Notes:
This expressive self portrait was painted during the peak of a severe psychotic episode. The progression of the artist's mental state is discernible in different areas of the work. Confusion and discord are apparent in the bold expressive strokes around the figure's face.

The eyes are very much the focus of the piece; picked out in glossy nail polish, which catch the light and draw the viewer to uncomfortably meet the figure's gaze.

2018-Tension_1_of_3-Studio-Painting.Title: Tension 1 of 3
2018-Tension_1_of_3-Studio-Painting.Private Notes:
This piece is the first part of a triptych about love, sexuality, and mortality. The first piece depicts a heterosexual couple. As the figures are cropped, they are depersonalised, and can be seen in a more conceptual sense, rather than as characters.

The raw, unprimed plywood shows through much of the image, giving a natural, organic feel to the flesh of the figures. The golden section between the figures has been scratched and roughened to add a texture which catches the light, and makes this piece truly a beautiful object.

2018-Tension_2_of_3-Studio-Painting.Title: Tension 2 of 3
2018-Tension_2_of_3-Studio-Painting.Private Notes:
This piece is the second part of a triptych about love, sexuality, and mortality. The second piece depicts a couple, just bones. Without skin and flesh, the figures are genderless. As the figures are cropped, they are depersonalised, and can be seen in a more conceptual sense, rather than as characters.

Less of the plywood shows through in this piece than in the other two. The golden section between the figures is bright and lustrous.

2018-Tension_3_of_3-Studio-Painting.Title: Tension 3 of 3
2018-Tension_3_of_3-Studio-Painting.Private Notes:
This piece is the third part of a triptych about love, sexuality, and mortality. The third piece depicts a gay couple. As the figures are cropped, they are depersonalised, and can be seen in a more conceptual sense, rather than as characters.

The raw, unprimed plywood shows through much of the image, giving a natural, organic feel to the flesh of the figures. The golden section between the figures has been scratched and roughened to add a texture, and was painted with a mixture of oil paint, glittery nail polish, and gold glitter, which catch the light, and make this piece truly a beautiful object.

2018-The_Duchess-Studio-Painting.Title: The Duchess
2018-The_Duchess-Studio-Painting.Private Notes: This double-sided piece was originally exhibited in the 2018 exhibition "Tension". It depicts the power and strength in feminine sexuality, with decorative male nudes.

2018-Two_Thousand_Five-Studio-Painting.Title: Two Thousand Five
2018-Two_Thousand_Five-Studio-Painting.Private Notes:
2005 was the year of BerriBlue's emigration from Poland to Ireland. It was a very important year, and a difficult one, marking the end of her childhood. Being uprooted so abruptly, and cutting off the life she had enjoyed had a profound effect on her.

The piece was featured in Attitude Interior Design Magazine's 2018 Nov/Dec edition. It is now in a private collection in The Netherlands.

2018-You_and_Me-Studio-Painting.Title: You and Me
2018-You_and_Me-Studio-Painting.Private Notes: This piece is part of a series exploring the fleeting nature of life. Love is eternal, but we are not.

2018-You,_Me,_and_the_Dog-Studio-Paintin.Title: You, Me, and the Dog
2018-You,_Me,_and_the_Dog-Studio-Paintin.Private Notes: This piece is part of a series exploring the fleeting nature of life. Love is eternal, but we are not.

2018-You,_Me,_the_Dog,_and_the_Bird-Stud.Title: You, Me, the Dog, and the Bird
2018-You,_Me,_the_Dog,_and_the_Bird-Stud.Display Name: You, Me, the Dog, and the Bird
2018-You,_Me,_the_Dog,_and_the_Bird-Stud.Private Notes: This piece is part of a series exploring the fleeting nature of life. Love is eternal, but we are not.

2019-Death-Street-Azulejo-Porto.Title: Death
2019-Death-Street-Azulejo-Porto.Private Notes: My first large Azulejo street art mural.

2019-Disjointed-Studio-Painting.Title: Disjointed
2019-Disjointed-Studio-Painting.Private Notes:
This piece was originally exhibited in the 2019 exhibition “The Fish & The Horse”, in which Berriblue explored and traced her visual inheritance. 
The simple, delicate, illustrative imagery belies the much more challenging subtext of mental health issues, disassociation, and self harm.

2019-Flowerbed-Studio-Painting.Title: Flowerbed
2019-Flowerbed-Studio-Painting.Private Notes:
This piece was originally exhibited in the 2019 exhibition “The Fish & The Horse”, in which Berriblue explored and traced her visual inheritance. 
The disturbing imagery is presented in a highly decorative way. The pain and visceral vanitas of life being present, hidden in plain sight. Not something shameful necessarily, but capable of being though of as beautiful, in its own way.

2019-Gutted-Studio-Painting.Title: Gutted
2019-Gutted-Studio-Painting.Private Notes:
This piece was originally exhibited in the 2019 exhibition “The Fish & The Horse”, in which Berriblue explored and traced her visual inheritance. 
This highly sexually charged piece expresses the carnal, animal beauty in masturbation and the subtlety of eroticism.

2019-Motyl-Studio-Painting.Title: Motyl
2019-Motyl-Studio-Painting.Private Notes:
This largely subconscious piece presents an interesting glimpse into artist's makings. It was originally exhibited in the 2019 exhibition “The Fish & The Horse”, in which Berriblue explored and traced her visual inheritance. 
The flowers and wild strawberries hold a personal, sentimental importance, evocative of childhood. The skull is representative of the artist's baby brother, who was lost in utero.

2019-The_Horse-Studio-Painting.Title: The Horse
2019-The_Horse-Studio-Painting.Private Notes: This piece was orignialy exhibited in 2019

2020-Fishes_1_and_2-Studio-Azulejo.Title: Fishes 1 and 2
2020-Fishes_1_and_2-Studio-Azulejo.Private Notes:
These two-by-one-tile pieces are ready for mounting & framing or to be incorporated into a tiled surface.
They are playful and simple, and yet retain a visceral complexity on closer inspection, with a beautiful interplay of textures and colours.
The traditional blue and white azulejo colours are picked out with vibrant reds.

2020-Knife_and_Sea_Cucumbers-Studio-Azul.Title: Knife and Sea Cucumbers
2020-Knife_and_Sea_Cucumbers-Studio-Azul.Private Notes:
This two-by-four tile mural is ready for mounting & framing or to be incorporated into a tiled surface.
The traditional blue and white azulejo motif is picked out in vibrant textural reds.
The playfully flat perspective creates a surreal, abstract atmosphere.

2021-Coming_Home-Studio-Painting.Title: Coming Home
2021-Coming_Home-Studio-Painting.Private Notes: A reaction to the pain and disappointment of a breakup, following a brief but significant absence / family

2021-Fish_II-Studio-Azulejo.Title: Fish II
2021-Fish_II-Studio-Azulejo.Private Notes:
Azulejo piece depicting a gutted fish. Framed in raw pine
The piece is executed in azulejos, traditional Portuguese ceramic tilework.

Imagery of fish, particularly sardines, are very common in Portugal.
The viscerality of the gutted fish is almost sexual.
The raw exposed chacota ceramic is something that's not often seen in azulejos, but is used here to contrast and compliment the more traditional blue and white.

2021-Kochanie_w_Czasach_Zarazy-Studio-Pa.Title: Kochanie w Czasach Zarazy
2021-Kochanie_w_Czasach_Zarazy-Studio-Pa.Private Notes: A revisited evaluation of a relationship, and its emotional toll, over the course of an extraordinarily difficult year.

2021-Przenikanie-Studio-Azulejo.Title: Przenikanie
2021-Przenikanie-Studio-Azulejo.Private Notes: A self portrait extending through past, present, and future selves, and the connection between them.

2022-Battle_Flag-Studio-Azulejo.Title: Battle Flag
2022-Battle_Flag-Studio-Azulejo.Private Notes: This azulejo piece embraces the tessellating nature of works in tiles, with broken patterns and mismatched shapes. The imagery is evocative of medieval standard banners; visceral animalistic elements balanced against abstract shapes.

2022-Choices-Studio-Azulejo.Title: Choices
2022-Choices-Studio-Azulejo.Private Notes: Azulejo piece framed in raw pine.

2022-Details_of_a_Lifetime_(Shrine_to_Se.Title: Details of a Lifetime (Shrine to Self)
2022-Details_of_a_Lifetime_(Shrine_to_Se.Display Name: Details of a Lifetime (Shrine to Self)
2022-Details_of_a_Lifetime_(Shrine_to_Se.Private Notes: This large mural expresses a sense of one's own mortality, and the elements that compose your existence. A sister piece was pasted up on Escadas do Pinheiro, Porto.

2022-Embrace-Studio-Azulejo.Title: Embrace
2022-Embrace-Studio-Azulejo.Private Notes:
This piece features two interlaced images, entwined in a dichotomic embrace.
The piece is executed in azulejos, traditional Portuguese ceramic tilework, and framed in raw pine.

The top section was originally two pieces, one showing the comforting embrace of the figure of death, the other showing a vicious clash of two daemon-like dog figures. The combination of both pieces expresses the sense of fear and disquiet in acknowledging one's mortal form, and our fragility in an ever more perilous world.
The hands reaching up from the bottom attempt to grasp at something, but are separated from the top image.

2022-Fish_III-Studio-Azulejo.Title: Fish III
2022-Fish_III-Studio-Azulejo.Private Notes:
Azulejo piece depicting a gutted fish. Framed in raw pine
The piece is executed in azulejos, traditional Portuguese ceramic tilework.

Imagery of fish, particularly sardines, are very common in Portugal.
The viscerality of the gutted fish is almost sexual.
The raw exposed chacota ceramic is something that's not often seen in azulejos, but is used here to contrast and compliment the more traditional blue and white.

2022-Flesh_and_Bone-Studio-Azulejo.Title: Flesh and Bone
2022-Flesh_and_Bone-Studio-Azulejo.Private Notes: Azulejo piece, framed in raw pine

2022-Kobieta-Studio-Azulejo.Title: Kobieta
2022-Kobieta-Studio-Azulejo.Private Notes:
​Kobieta, Polish for "Woman", executed in traditional Portuguese azulejos.

This large azulejo piece, framed in raw pine, depicts an incomplete female form, pierced in the heart and womb. The combination of bare ceramic, raw wood, and glaze in various textures and vibrant colours come together in a very imposing, expressive way.

The disembodied female figure is a theme I use a lot in my work. There is a very visceral feeling to image, which embodies a sense of carnal womanhood, but in a sense robbed of its personhood. Spears pierce the figure in the womb and in the heart.

The strips of traditional blue and blood red in the background clash on a central line, broken up by the body in raw chacota ceramic, given a thin clear glaze to bring out the natural lustre in the material.

At the bottom of the piece, there is a collection of stylised skulls. The representation is obviously of death and mortality, but there is also a sense of the connection between the living self and the corporeal body, which is inherently mortal, and evoking a sense of vanitas.

The piece stands almost 2 metres tall, and is intended to be hung at least 1.5m from the ground, so that it looms high over the viewer, bringing to mind medieval Polish icons, often incorporating gruesome wooden statues, with a huge and solemn presence.

2022-Rua_das_Flores-Sister_Piece-Studio-.Title: Rua das Flores - Sister Piece
2022-Rua_das_Flores-Sister_Piece-Studio-.Private Notes: This azuejo piece was commissioned as a sister piece to the street art mural on Rua das Flores

2022-Santo_Antonio-Studio-Azulejo.Title: Santo Antonio
2022-Santo_Antonio-Studio-Azulejo.Private Notes:
This piece is not named for its subject matter, but for the location it was originally intended for as a street art piece.
Before being put up on the street, it was purchased for a private collection.

2022-Skulls-Street-Azulejo-Various.Title: Skulls
2022-Skulls-Street-Azulejo-Various.Private Notes:
These pieces were part of an azulejo project in the summer of 2022.
Many individual pieces were placed in cities across Poland.

2022-The_Fish-Studio-Azulejo.Title: The Fish
2022-The_Fish-Studio-Azulejo.Private Notes: Azulejo piece depicting a gutted fish. Framed in raw pine

2022-Waiting-Studio-Azulejo.Title: Waiting
2022-Waiting-Studio-Azulejo.Private Notes: Azulejo piece, framed in raw pine.

2023-Cat_WWA-Street-Azulejo-Warsaw.Title: Cat WWA
2023-Cat_WWA-Street-Azulejo-Warsaw.Private Notes: A sister piece to the one in Paris.

2023-Dogs-Street-Azulejo-Warsaw.Title: Dogs
2023-Dogs-Street-Azulejo-Warsaw.Private Notes: These small azulejo pieces appear in various locations in Poland and Portugal

2023-Fishbowl-Studio-Painting.Title: Fishbowl
2023-Fishbowl-Studio-Painting.Medium: Paint, Pastel, and Nails on Plywood

2023-Kot_II-Studio-Azulejo.Title: Kot II
2023-Kot_II-Studio-Azulejo.Private Notes:
A sister to the street art pieces in Warsaw and Paris.
Framed and mounted.

2023-My_Pigeon_was_Killed_by_Cats-Studio.Title: My Pigeon was Killed by Cats
2023-My_Pigeon_was_Killed_by_Cats-Studio.Display Name: My Pigeon was Killed by Cats

2023-Paris_Kot-Street-Azulejo-Paris.Title: Paris Kot
2023-Paris_Kot-Street-Azulejo-Paris.Private Notes: A sister piece to the one in Warsaw.

2024-Dandelion_(azulejo)-Studio-Azulejo.Title: Dandelion (azulejo)
2024-Dandelion_(azulejo)-Studio-Azulejo.Private Notes: This piece has a sister piece executed on canvas

2024-Dandelion_(painting)-Studio-Paintin.Title: Dandelion (painting)
2024-Dandelion_(painting)-Studio-Paintin.Private Notes: This piece has a sister piece executed in azulejo
